Nandurra Grid Systems Limited (AEX:XX00, ABN 00 000 000 000) — Data Retention and DSAR Workflow (Australia).
We collect personal information under the Privacy Act 1988 (Cth) for HR, payroll, HSE, trade-credit assessment, and customer support; opinions recorded in performance notes may identify an individual.
Routine records include role-based emails (e.g., finance@ngsl.example.au), employee identifiers (EID-0000-XYZ), contractor onboarding checks, and training completions (cyber, tipping, and information-barrier modules).
Marketing examples reference crypto and ESG topics in education only; our “Token Basics for Utilities” webinar does not contain transaction data or investment advice.
Insiders and blackout windows are explained in generic policy guides for staff share trading and do not disclose deal-stage information.
Data is stored in AU-hosted services with backups; transfers outside Australia use standard contracts and vendor DPIAs; no overseas disclosure is made without notice.
DSAR process: lodge via privacy@ngsl.example.au; we verify identity and respond within a reasonable period; redactions apply to third-party confidentiality and legal privilege.
Employment and health/safety records are retained for statutory periods (e.g., incident reports, workers compensation files); credit files for trade accounts are kept for audit cycles then securely destroyed.
Security incidents are logged for tabletop drills, and example breach playbooks use placeholder account numbers (e.g., ACC-9999-TEST) and invalid IDs, not live customer data.
A retention hold is applied to a draft ESG-linked power purchase agreement for a microgrid project pending national exchange release; access is ring-fenced under an information barrier and recorded on the insider list.
Routine commercial negotiations disclosed publicly on 2025-12-01 remain outside this hold; published summaries on our investor page and the market operator’s website are already generally available information.
On 2026-05-20 a preliminary cyber incident review flagged test-user access to a non-public crypto-custody integration runbook including draft commercial term ranges; content was quarantined pending internal materiality review under the Corporations Act.
We prohibit personal trading discussions on internal channels and remind staff that training scenarios about tipping and crypto tokens are hypothetical.
Complaints may be escalated to the national privacy regulator; this note is informational and does not create contractual rights.
